ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Saulo ebisi asɛ, “Apalete, wonu afana?” Ebuiliulũ akɔɛ asɛ, “Mɛinu Yesu, anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yiede wɔtɔnɔɔ.”
translated: Saulo asked him, “Apalete, are you not a soldier?” Ebuiliulũ replied, “I am Jesus, I have come to save the world.”

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Kɛlɛ nɔmɔ̀ pɛ nɔolũ kana gɛ Paulo anyɛ alɛ etsyina banunɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ, pɛ agbana banɔkpekpèɛ atsaɖakɛ idzo nɛ Efosoɔ nɛ̀ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ petee tsyɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ ibudiɛ gɛ banɔdzyrameɛ abhɛtɛ tinu akaa nanà.
translated: The reason why Paul decided to go to Jerusalem despite everything was that he wanted to complete the collection for the poor saints in Judea and Asia. He considered this to be a very important duty.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: Wɔbodzì anɔ gɛ ɔbɔɖɔaɖɔ ɔdalɛ yɛishu ɔkɔ banunɔ petee nɛ kɛgɛ ɔmɔ pɛ olũɔ shu.
translated: They say that when the sun sets, all animals go to their homes and the human being also goes to sleep.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: Gɛ belũ ozĩ ɔlɛɛ, banɔɛza gbagbla tili bashogò kɛlɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛibi, ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ elũ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔɔ nɛ yɛulũhũ mɛ.
translated: When the child was born, they placed him in a manger because there was no room for them in the inn.

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: Kesiakpa abha ekusiɛ batɔ̃ kɛtakpu kɛlɛ beekusiɛ beshogò nɛ Apaleteɛ shu nɛ̀ agɛ yɛasara bɔbɔ ɔkɔɔ,
translated: The elders said that if a child is stubborn and does not listen, when Apaleteɛ comes, he will take the child away.

ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Saulo ebisi asɛ, “Apalete, wonu afana?” Ebuiliulũ akɔɛ asɛ, “Mɛinu Yesu, anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yiede wɔtɔnɔɔ.”
translated: Saulo asked, “Apalete, are you going?” Ebuiliulũ replied, “I am going to Jesus, I will not stay here anymore.”

translated: The people who were with him saw what Paul was doing and they all shouted loudly, saying that the gods had come down to them in the form of humans. But this did not please the people of the city.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: Wɔbodzì anɔ gɛ ɔbɔɖɔaɖɔ ɔdalɛ yɛishu ɔkɔ banunɔ petee nɛ kɛgɛ ɔmɔ pɛ olũɔ shu.
translated: They say that when the moon is full, it rises and covers all the stars and even the sun itself.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: Gɛ belũ ozĩ ɔlɛɛ, banɔɛza gbagbla tili bashogò kɛlɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛibi, ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ elũ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔɔ nɛ yɛulũhũ mɛ.
translated: When the child is sick, the mother carries him on her back to the hospital, but she does not know that the child will die.

translated: The children went to the farm to harvest cassava and then returned home to prepare food for Apaleteɛ and her husband who had gone hunting.

translated: Paulo and his companions went to the region of Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit prevented them from speaking the word in Asia. Then they attempted to go to Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”

translated: The children went to the farm and when they returned they said that Apaleteɛ had gone to the river to fetch water.

ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Saulo ebisi asɛ, “Apalete, wonu afana?” Ebuiliulũ akɔɛ asɛ, “Mɛinu Yesu, anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yiede wɔtɔnɔɔ.”
translated: Saulo asked, “Apalete, are you going?” Ebuiliulũ replied, “I am going to Jesus, he has called me to follow him.”

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Kɛlɛ nɔmɔ̀ pɛ nɔolũ kana gɛ Paulo anyɛ alɛ etsyina banunɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ, pɛ agbana banɔkpekpèɛ atsaɖakɛ idzo nɛ Efosoɔ nɛ̀ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ petee tsyɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ ibudiɛ gɛ banɔdzyrameɛ abhɛtɛ tinu akaa nanà.
translated: The man named Paulo who lives in the town of Etsyi has been suffering from a severe illness for a long time, and his condition has worsened so much that people from Efoso and Asia have all come to see him. They say he is about to die.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: Wɔbodzì anɔ gɛ ɔbɔɖɔaɖɔ ɔdalɛ yɛishu ɔkɔ banunɔ petee nɛ kɛgɛ ɔmɔ pɛ olũɔ shu.
translated: They say that when the tortoise walks, it does not rush but takes its time to reach wherever it is going.

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: Kliso bɔlɔ petee badɛmɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bubu banɔ teli ɔkpɛ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ aplɛnɔ bɔlɔ ɔbhia alɛ gɛ bɔdɛmɛ nɔ gɛenu Barnaba nɛ̀ Paulo,
translated: All the people who were in the church listened to the things that were said, and they decided that Barnabas and Paul should go.

ref: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
text: Banɔ kasasà tieli gɛedzi behuinɛ tsyɛ alɔkɔ balɛ ihuiɛ amana pɛ bapɔɛ ɛlɛ nɛ ɔmaɛshi. Gɛ bakalà ehoeyiɛ gɛ bezĩ nɛ balɛ akpɛnɛ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ shuɔ, labanà sikãfufu ihotu afɛeti, gɛenu ehoeyi gɛ lavɛ otugbà nɛ kibè ɛlɛ.
translated: They say that in the past, the people of Kasasa used to live in the forest, and no one could enter their land. One day, a hunter went into the forest and saw a woman grinding cassava, and he took her and brought her to his house.

ref: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
text: Butizĩ kibè bafala nɔ, kɛlɛ kelĩabha idzo gagã tsyɛ zikuifù, ɔmɔsɛ olugbũ nyanyã gɛonu Tɛdɔdɔfala Kiwuiɛ tɛfala. Kliso Paulo akpɛ ɔshà akɔ alɛ asɛ,
translated: The people of Butizi were farmers, but because of the war they fled and left their homes. They crossed the Togo border at Kiwuiɛ and settled there. Pastor Paulo came and met them there.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: Krispo, anɔ gɛ eɖi ɔtsyɔakɔ nabha, nɛ̀ yɛɔpã petee yɛkɛ Apalete nabha aŋã, kɛlɛ Korintoanɛ kpekpè teli gɛ belũ yɛɔdɔ ayɛkɛ aŋã kɛlɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ.
translated: Krispo, when he was in the world, and all the people of Apalete were in the world, the Corinthians were also in the world and they were doing evil.

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: Nɛ ebui alɛ niesi bubú ɔkɔ alɛ tsyɛ elo, kɛgagalĩ ɔkɔ Barnaba nɛ̀ Paulo tɛ betsodzì banɔɛza asɛ batɛpɔɛ bɔdɔ̃ akɔ alɛ.
translated: When they had finished speaking in the synagogue of the Jews, many of the Jews and the devout Greeks, as many as had believed, followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them, urging them to continue in the grace of God.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: Gɛ belũ ozĩ ɔlɛɛ, banɔɛza gbagbla tili bashogò kɛlɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛibi, ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ elũ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔɔ nɛ yɛulũhũ mɛ.

translated: The elders said that when Paulo went to that village, he caused a great disturbance, and the people gathered together to stone him and Barnabas, who were from the regions of Efoso and Asia. But they fled and went to another place.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.

translated: They say that when the moon is full, it brings light to the whole world and everyone sees clearly.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.

translated: When the child was born, the elders went to the diviner to find out what name to give him, and they were told that he should be named after the river because he came from the water.

translated: Paulo had already decided in his heart not to pass through Corinth again, but to go through Macedonia after leaving there, and then return to Asia. He did this so that no one would think he was being inconsistent.

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.

translated: They said that if a person does not work, they will not eat any food and their body will become thin.

ref: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
text: Gɛ bediku ɔma mɛɛ, bewu avɛ kabha buvũɔ mɛ gɛ bazazãa. Bagɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ balɛ enu Petro, Yohanes, Yakobo nɛ̀ Andrea, Filipo nɛ̀ Toma, Bartolomeo nɛ̀ Mateo, Yakobo, Alfeo yɛeyiɛ nɛ̀ Simon, Ishuahɔɛmɛ zazã pɛpɛelete Kɛzamɛanɔ, nɛ̀ Yuda Yakobo yɛeyiɛ.
translated: When they arrived there, they saw a crowd around them and the teachers of the law arguing with them. And immediately all the people, when they saw him, were greatly amazed and ran up to him and greeted him. And he asked them, “What are you arguing about with them?” And someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I brought my son to you, for he has a spirit that makes him mute. And whenever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid. So I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they were not able.” And he answered them, “O faithless generation, how long am I to be with you? How long am I to bear with you? Bring him to me.” And they brought the boy to him. And when the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth. And Jesus asked his father, “How long has this been happening to him?” And he said, “From childhood. And it has often cast him into fire and into water, to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.” Jesus said to him, “‘If you can’! All things are possible for one who believes.” Immediately the father of the child cried out and said, “I believe; help my unbelief!” And when Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, “You mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him and never enter him again.” And after crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, “He is dead.” But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he arose. And when he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why could we not cast it out?” And he said to them, “This kind cannot be driven out by anything but prayer.”
